meta.setting: Klamath River routes, forested canyon roads, and northern Jefferson border country
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with river travel, land pressure, family work, negotiation, and hard frontier choices
meta.quality_bar: hand-cut, grounded, respectful, table-useful, western-only language

research_grounding.title: Karuk Tribe
research_grounding.use: Klamath River ancestral lands, salmon fishing, basketry, environmental stewardship, cultural burning, language and tradition preservation, and self-governance.

research_grounding.title: Yurok Tribe
research_grounding.use: Sovereign rights, self-governance, cultural preservation, land and water stewardship, reciprocity, and dignity.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Karuk River Families cast page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: square image reference immediately after the title
opening blockquote with invented respectful river note or travel observation generated later; do not use fake Karuk language
H2 archetype subtitle in the register of Klamath River Households
one or two compact lore paragraphs introducing families and river workers tied to salmon, burning, travel, trade, and self-governance along the Klamath
H3 Role with a short table-facing function phrase
H3 Traits with two to four grounded signs, strengths, or social facts
10 H4 headings, each followed later by a brief playable move, leverage, cost, edge, or complication
H3 Camp Say closing blockquote with plain meaning generated later
generation_guidance: Write this as a grounded tribal community page connected to the Klamath River and regional travel.
Tie future moves to river knowledge, stewardship, food systems, negotiation, warning, route permission, family protection, and outsider obligations.
Let the page show pressure from miners, roads, timber, law, and outsiders without reducing people to victims or side characters.
Do not invent sacred details, fake words, decorative costumes, or special powers.
Do not include final move names or final move text in this JSON.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: The page should move like river work: practiced, alert, and unwilling to be hurried by a stranger.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A river crossing can be a trade route, a boundary, a pantry, and a courtroom all before noon.
must_preserve: Use respectful 1899 western and regional language only.
Do not invent sacred practices, fake words, or mystical traits.
Represent Native people as living families, workers, stewards, negotiators, and political actors.
The generated page must include image path cast/images/karuk-river-families.jpg.
No finished page prose, final moves, or final sayings belong in this prompt file.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not include medieval, enchanted, sacred-invented, or fake-tribal framing
do not reduce Native people to victims, guides, costumes, or symbols
do not use modern slang, modern gear, vehicles, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is respectful, grounded, and useful for play.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to 1899 Jefferson State western play and respects living Native communities.
